summary: A Jest test failed because the registry URL returned by the command includes a trailing slash while the test expects the URL without a trailing slash.
root_cause: A test in test/config/get.ts asserted the exact string "https://my-org.registry.example.com" but the command output was "https://my-org.registry.example.com/" (trailing slash), causing an equality assertion to fail.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific failing test case and its description.
The test expected the registry URL without a trailing slash.
The command output returned the registry URL with a trailing slash, which caused the equality assertion to fail.

suggested_fix: Either normalize the compared values to remove a trailing slash before asserting (e.g. compare stdout.trim().replace(/\/$/, '')), or update the code that prints the registry URL to consistently omit the trailing slash so the test expectation matches the output.
